title: Battle of Bands
shortDescription: Unleash your musical talent in this epic rock & pop showdown. Solo artists and bands welcome!
description: Battle of Bands is the flagship cultural event of Utsaha Vaibhava. This high-energy competition invites musicians from across colleges to showcase their talent in rock, pop, fusion, and indie genres. Expect an electric crowd, professional sound systems, and an unforgettable night of music!
rules: Own instruments must be brought; PA system will be provided
No pre-recorded tracks (backing tracks allowed for specific instruments only)
Vulgar or offensive content is strictly prohibited
eligibility: Open to all college students with valid ID proof

title: Code Odyssey
shortDescription: A 4-hour coding marathon spanning algorithms, data structures, and real-world problem solving.
description: Code Odyssey is the ultimate competitive programming challenge at Utsaha Vaibhava. Participants will tackle progressively harder algorithmic questions within a time limit. The event tests speed, accuracy, and creative thinking. Problems span multiple domains including dynamic programming, graph theory, and system design.
rules: Languages allowed: C, C++, Java, Python
Use of AI tools or code generators is not allowed
All solutions will be tested on hidden test cases
eligibility: UG/PG students of any engineering branch

title: Nritya Sangam
shortDescription: A classical and contemporary dance competition celebrating India's rich dance heritage.
description: Nritya Sangam is a vibrant dance competition that celebrates both classical and contemporary Indian dance forms. From Bharatanatyam to Bollywood fusion, this event is a visual feast of grace, expression, and energy. Groups and soloists are both welcome to perform.
rules: Music must be submitted 48 hours in advance
Entry in Indian classical or contemporary forms only

title: Hackathon 24
shortDescription: A 24-hour hackathon — build innovative solutions for real-world problems. Mentors and goodies included!
description: Hackathon 24 is Utsaha Vaibhava's flagship technical event — a 24-hour non-stop innovation challenge where teams build functional prototypes addressing real-world problems in domains like Healthcare, EdTech, FinTech, and Smart Cities. Industry mentors, cloud credits, and amazing goodies await!
rules: Work must be original and built during the hackathon period
Teams must present a working prototype at the end
Use of public APIs and open-source libraries is allowed
Plagiarism will lead to immediate disqualification
All domains welcome; bonus for social impact projects

title: Cricket Blitz
shortDescription: A thrilling T10 inter-college cricket tournament on the college's turf ground.
description: Cricket Blitz is a fast-paced T10 cricket tournament where college teams compete for glory on the field. With 10-over matches and knockout rounds, the action never stops. Cheer squads, live commentary, and trophies for winners!
eligibility: Male students from any college with valid ID

title: Gaming Arena
shortDescription: BGMI & Free Fire esports tournament — compete for the Chicken Dinner and cash prizes!
description: Gaming Arena is the most electrifying esports event at Utsaha Vaibhava! Compete in BGMI (Battlegrounds Mobile India) and Free Fire squad tournaments for glory and cash prizes. High-end gaming setup, live streaming, and a roaring crowd make this a truly esports-grade experience.
rules: Accounts must be at least Diamond rank for BGMI
Hacking or use of mods leads to immediate ban
eligibility: All college students with valid ID

title: Robo Rumble
shortDescription: Design, build, and battle your robot in India's most exciting college robotics competition.
description: Robo Rumble is the engineering marvel of Utsaha Vaibhava. Teams design and build autonomous or semi-autonomous robots and compete in an obstacle course and combat arena. This event tests mechanical design, electronics, and programming skills — all in one thrilling package.
rules: Robots must not exceed 25kg in weight
No flammable or corrosive substances allowed
Remote-controlled and autonomous bots both allowed
Each team gets 2 matches in the arena
